critical: All 5 configurations failed with runtime errors; no accuracy scores were produced. The experiment yields zero valid results and cannot support any model comparison or winner selection.
winner_config_id is null because all configs failed — no winner can be declared. The experiment must be rerun before any conclusion is possible.

The target_column field in the experiment contract is a tab-separated string of all column headers rather than a single column name. This malformed input caused the preprocessing pipeline to strip all features, resulting in shape=(156, 0) for every configuration.

The primary metric (accuracy) was never evaluated for any configuration. The failure_threshold of 0.85 cannot be assessed. There is zero evidence that RandomForest on this dataset meets or fails the threshold.
warnings: The pipeline lacks defensive input validation: a feature-shape assertion (e.g., assert X.shape[1] > 0) after preprocessing would have surfaced the CSV parsing bug immediately rather than allowing all five configs to reach StandardScaler with an empty feature matrix.

The debate summary claims the root cause is a malformed target_column field — this is a plausible diagnosis but unverified without inspecting the actual CSV or pipeline code. The report should confirm this explicitly before rerunning.
revised_recommendation: The experiment must not be accepted in its current state. Corrective steps required before rerunning: (1) Fix the target_column field in the experiment contract to contain only the single target column name (e.g., 'Survived'), not a tab-separated header string. (2) Verify that the CSV at the specified path is correctly formatted and readable. (3) Add a post-preprocessing assertion that X.shape[1] > 0 to fail fast on empty feature matrices. (4) Rerun all 5 configurations from scratch and report accuracy scores, mean/std across CV folds, and train vs. test metrics to check for overfitting. Only after obtaining valid results can a winner be declared and compared against the 0.85 failure threshold.
